lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਪੰਖ ਪਰਾਹੁਣੀ ਦੁਨੀ ਸੁਹਾਵਾ ਬਾਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa pankh paraahunee dunee suhaavaa baag |
lines.english: Fareed, the bird is a guest in this beautiful world-garden.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹੀ ਰੋਗੁ ਨ ਲਗਈ ਪਲੈ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਪਾਇ ॥੭੮॥
lines.translit: dehee rog na lagee palai sabh kichh paae |78|
lines.english: Your body shall not suffer from any disease, and you shall obtain everything. ||78||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਉਬਤਿ ਵਜੀ ਸੁਬਹ ਸਿਉ ਚਲਣ ਕਾ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਜੁ ॥੭੯॥
lines.translit: naubat vajee subah siau chalan kaa kar saaj |79|
lines.english: The morning drums are beating - get ready to leave! ||79||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰਾਤਿ ਕਥੂਰੀ ਵੰਡੀਐ ਸੁਤਿਆ ਮਿਲੈ ਨ ਭਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa raat kathooree vanddeeai sutiaa milai na bhaau |
lines.english: Fareed, musk is released at night. Those who are sleeping do not receive their share.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮੈ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਦੁਖੁ ਮੁਝ ਕੂ ਦੁਖੁ ਸਬਾਇਐ ਜਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa mai jaaniaa dukh mujh koo dukh sabaaeiai jag |
lines.english: Fareed, I thought that I was in trouble; the whole world is in trouble!

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿੰਨੑਾ ਨੈਣ ਨਂ‍ੀਦ੍ਰਾਵਲੇ ਤਿੰਨੑਾ ਮਿਲਣੁ ਕੁਆਉ ॥੮੦॥
lines.translit: jinaa nain naneedraavale tinaa milan kuaau |80|
lines.english: Those whose eyes are heavy with sleep - how can they receive it? ||80||

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਚੇ ਚੜਿ ਕੈ ਦੇਖਿਆ ਤਾਂ ਘਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਏਹਾ ਅਗਿ ॥੮੧॥
lines.translit: aooche charr kai dekhiaa taan ghar ghar ehaa ag |81|
lines.english: When I climbed the hill and looked around, I saw this fire in each and every home. ||81||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਭੂਮਿ ਰੰਗਾਵਲੀ ਮੰਝਿ ਵਿਸੂਲਾ ਬਾਗ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa bhoom rangaavalee manjh visoolaa baag |
lines.english: Fareed, in the midst of this beautiful earth, there is a garden of thorns.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨ ਪੀਰਿ ਨਿਵਾਜਿਆ ਤਿੰਨੑਾ ਅੰਚ ਨ ਲਾਗ ॥੮੨॥
lines.translit: jo jan peer nivaajiaa tinaa anch na laag |82|
lines.english: Those humble beings who are blessed by their spiritual teacher, do not suffer even a scratch. ||82||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਉਮਰ ਸੁਹਾਵੜੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਸੁਵੰਨੜੀ ਦੇਹ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa umar suhaavarree sang suvanarree deh |
lines.english: Fareed, life is blessed and beautiful, along with the beautiful body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਰਲੇ ਕੇਈ ਪਾਈਅਨਿ ਜਿੰਨੑਾ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਨੇਹ ॥੮੩॥
lines.translit: virale keee paaeean jinaa piaare neh |83|
lines.english: Only a rare few are found, who love their Beloved Lord. ||83||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਡੁਖਾ ਸੇਤੀ ਦਿਹੁ ਗਇਆ ਸੂਲਾਂ ਸੇਤੀ ਰਾਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa ddukhaa setee dihu geaa soolaan setee raat |
lines.english: Fareed, the day passes painfully; the night is spent in anguish.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਧਰਿ ਰਬ ਰਜਾਇ ਵਹਣੁ ਤਿਦਾਊ ਗੰਉ ਕਰੇ ॥੮੪॥
lines.translit: jidhar rab rajaae vehan tidaaoo gnau kare |84|
lines.english: The river flows in whatever direction the Lord orders. ||84||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਧੀ ਵਹਣ ਨ ਢਾਹਿ ਤਉ ਭੀ ਲੇਖਾ ਦੇਵਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: kandhee vehan na dtaeh tau bhee lekhaa devanaa |
lines.english: O river, do not destroy your banks; you too will be asked to give your account.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੜਾ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ਪਾਤਣੀ ਬੇੜਾ ਕਪਰ ਵਾਤਿ ॥੮੫॥
lines.translit: kharraa pukaare paatanee berraa kapar vaat |85|
lines.english: The boatman stands up and shouts, "The boat is caught in the whirlpool!" ||85||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੰਮੀ ਲੰਮੀ ਨਦੀ ਵਹੈ ਕੰਧੀ ਕੇਰੈ ਹੇਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: lamee lamee nadee vahai kandhee kerai het |
lines.english: The river flows on and on; it loves to eat into its banks.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇੜੇ ਨੋ ਕਪਰੁ ਕਿਆ ਕਰੇ ਜੇ ਪਾਤਣ ਰਹੈ ਸੁਚੇਤਿ ॥੮੬॥
lines.translit: berre no kapar kiaa kare je paatan rahai suchet |86|
lines.english: What can the whirlpool do to the boat, if the boatman remains alert? ||86||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਗਲਂ‍ੀ ਸੁ ਸਜਣ ਵੀਹ ਇਕੁ ਢੂੰਢੇਦੀ ਨ ਲਹਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa galanee su sajan veeh ik dtoondtedee na lahaan |
lines.english: Fareed, there are dozens who say they are friends; I search, but I cannot find even one.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਇਹੁ ਤਨੁ ਭਉਕਣਾ ਨਿਤ ਨਿਤ ਦੁਖੀਐ ਕਉਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa ihu tan bhaukanaa nit nit dukheeai kaun |
lines.english: Fareed, this body is always barking. Who can stand this constant suffering?

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਖਾਂ ਜਿਉ ਮਾਂਲੀਹ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਤਿੰਨੑਾ ਮਾ ਪਿਰੀ ॥੮੭॥
lines.translit: dhukhaan jiau maanleeh kaaran tinaa maa piree |87|
lines.english: I yearn for my beloved like a smouldering fire. ||87||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਨੀ ਬੁਜੇ ਦੇ ਰਹਾਂ ਕਿਤੀ ਵਗੈ ਪਉਣੁ ॥੮੮॥
lines.translit: kanee buje de rahaan kitee vagai paun |88|
lines.english: I have put plugs in my ears; I don't care how much the wind is blowing. ||88||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰਬ ਖਜੂਰੀ ਪਕੀਆਂ ਮਾਖਿਅ ਨਈ ਵਹੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa rab khajooree pakeean maakhia nee vahana |
lines.english: Fareed, God's dates have ripened, and rivers of honey flow.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਤਨੁ ਸੁਕਾ ਪਿੰਜਰੁ ਥੀਆ ਤਲੀਆਂ ਖੂੰਡਹਿ ਕਾਗ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa tan sukaa pinjar theea taleean khoonddeh kaag |
lines.english: Fareed, my withered body has become a skeleton; the crows are pecking at my palms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜੋ ਵੰਞੈਂ ਡੀਹੜਾ ਸੋ ਉਮਰ ਹਥ ਪਵੰਨਿ ॥੮੯॥
lines.translit: jo jo vanyain ddeeharraa so umar hath pavan |89|
lines.english: With each passing day, your life is being stolen away. ||89||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜੈ ਸੁ ਰਬੁ ਨ ਬਾਹੁੜਿਓ ਦੇਖੁ ਬੰਦੇ ਕੇ ਭਾਗ ॥੯੦॥
lines.translit: ajai su rab na baahurrio dekh bande ke bhaag |90|
lines.english: Even now, God has not come to help me; behold, this is the fate of all mortal beings. ||90||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਗਾ ਕਰੰਗ ਢੰਢੋਲਿਆ ਸਗਲਾ ਖਾਇਆ ਮਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: kaagaa karang dtandtoliaa sagalaa khaaeaa maas |
lines.english: The crows have searched my skeleton, and eaten all my flesh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏ ਦੁਇ ਨੈਨਾ ਮਤਿ ਛੁਹਉ ਪਿਰ ਦੇਖਨ ਕੀ ਆਸ ॥੯੧॥
lines.translit: e due nainaa mat chhuhau pir dekhan kee aas |91|
lines.english: But please do not touch these eyes; I hope to see my Lord. ||91||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਗਾ ਚੂੰਡਿ ਨ ਪਿੰਜਰਾ ਬਸੈ ਤ ਉਡਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: kaagaa choondd na pinjaraa basai ta uddar jaeh |
lines.english: O crow, do not peck at my skeleton; if you have landed on it, fly away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਪਿੰਜਰੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਸਹੁ ਵਸੈ ਮਾਸੁ ਨ ਤਿਦੂ ਖਾਹਿ ॥੯੨॥
lines.translit: jit pinjarai meraa sahu vasai maas na tidoo khaeh |92|
lines.english: Do not eat the flesh from that skeleton, within which my Husband Lord abides. ||92||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਗੋਰ ਨਿਮਾਣੀ ਸਡੁ ਕਰੇ ਨਿਘਰਿਆ ਘਰਿ ਆਉ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa gor nimaanee sadd kare nighariaa ghar aau |
lines.english: Fareed, the poor grave calls out, "O homeless one, come back to your home.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਪਰ ਮੈਥੈ ਆਵਣਾ ਮਰਣਹੁ ਨਾ ਡਰਿਆਹੁ ॥੯੩॥
lines.translit: sarapar maithai aavanaa maranahu naa ddariaahu |93|
lines.english: You shall surely have to come to me; do not be afraid of death." ||93||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਨੀ ਲੋਇਣੀ ਦੇਖਦਿਆ ਕੇਤੀ ਚਲਿ ਗਈ ॥
lines.translit: enee loeinee dekhadiaa ketee chal gee |
lines.english: These eyes have seen a great many leave.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਆਪੋ ਆਪਣੀ ਮੈ ਆਪਣੀ ਪਈ ॥੯੪॥
lines.translit: fareedaa lokaan aapo aapanee mai aapanee pee |94|
lines.english: Fareed, the people have their fate, and I have mine. ||94||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਸਵਾਰਹਿ ਮੈ ਮਿਲਹਿ ਮੈ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: aap savaareh mai mileh mai miliaa sukh hoe |
lines.english: God says, "If you reform yourself, you shall meet me, and meeting me, you shall be at peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਧੀ ਉਤੈ ਰੁਖੜਾ ਕਿਚਰਕੁ ਬੰਨੈ ਧੀਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kandhee utai rukharraa kicharak banai dheer |
lines.english: How long can the tree remain implanted on the river-bank?

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੇ ਤੂ ਮੇਰਾ ਹੋਇ ਰਹਹਿ ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੯੫॥
lines.translit: fareedaa je too meraa hoe raheh sabh jag teraa hoe |95|
lines.english: O Fareed, if you will be mine, the whole world will be yours." ||95||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮਹਲ ਨਿਸਖਣ ਰਹਿ ਗਏ ਵਾਸਾ ਆਇਆ ਤਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa mehal nisakhan reh ge vaasaa aaeaa tal |
lines.english: Fareed, the mansions are vacant; those who lived in them have gone to live underground.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਚੈ ਭਾਂਡੈ ਰਖੀਐ ਕਿਚਰੁ ਤਾਈ ਨੀਰੁ ॥੯੬॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kachai bhaanddai rakheeai kichar taaee neer |96|
lines.english: Fareed, how long can water be kept in a soft clay pot? ||96||
